summary: Finished matching sysdolphin/baselib/id.c by replacing the remaining inline assembly/non-matching block for HSD_IDInsertToTable with C, moving hash from a header macro into an inline C helper, adding an IDEntryAlloc helper, and adding id.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED. The PR had no review comments or body, so intent is inferred from the title and diff.
what_changed: Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/id.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk.
Removed static string labels lbl_804D5EE8 and lbl_804D5EF0 from id.c and replaced their use with direct string literals in __assert("id.c", 67, "entry").
Removed the #ifdef NON_MATCHING split and the full asm implementation of HSD_IDInsertToTable.

Implemented HSD_IDInsertToTable in C, including defaulting a NULL table to default_table, looking up an existing IDEntry by hash bucket, updating an existing entry if found, or allocating/inserting a new entry at the bucket head if absent.
Added inline u32 hash(u32 id) in id.c using id % 0x65.
Added inline IDEntry* IDEntryAlloc() to centralize HSD_ObjAlloc, NULL assertion, and memset initialization for IDEntry.
Removed #pragma push, #pragma peephole on, and #pragma pop around IDEntryFree/nearby code.
Removed the hash(s) macro definition from id.h.
smart_moves: Converted the hash macro into a C inline helper local to id.c, reducing header pollution while preserving the hash expression needed for matching.
Factored allocation plus assert plus zero-initialization into IDEntryAlloc, matching the repeated assembly behavior while making HSD_IDInsertToTable easier to shape.
Structured the search loop as an explicit while with an inner id equality break, which mirrors the assembly's branch pattern more directly than a compound loop condition.
Recomputed hash(id) at use sites instead of storing a local hash_key, likely to influence register allocation/control flow for the final match.

Used direct string literals for the assert arguments after removing small static label variables, indicating the compiler/linker placement still matched without keeping explicit lbl_804D5EE8/lbl_804D5EF0 objects.
decomp_lessons: When replacing an asm block, keep the C control flow close to the observed assembly: NULL table fallback, bucket selection, traversal, found-entry update path, and allocation/insertion path are separated clearly here.

For hash-table insertion routines, check whether the original updates an existing node versus always allocating; this PR's final C updates id/data if the id is already present and only allocates on a miss.
A macro in a header can be moved to an inline helper in the C file when it is only needed locally and when doing so helps matching or avoids exposing unnecessary definitions.
Small helper functions such as IDEntryAlloc can make object allocation patterns match while also documenting invariant work: HSD_ObjAlloc, assert on NULL, memset to sizeof(IDEntry).
After a source file matches, remember to update build/object lists such as EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED so the matched object is built in the intended category.
naming_conventions: Public baselib functions in this file use HSD_ID-prefixed names, including HSD_IDGetAllocData, HSD_IDSetup, and HSD_IDInsertToTable.
Local helpers use concise IDEntry-focused names such as IDEntryAlloc and IDEntryFree.
The local hash helper is lowercase hash and takes a u32 id.
The ID table entry type is IDEntry, with fields next, id, and data visible from the insertion logic.
The global allocator/table symbols are hsd_iddata and default_table.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed an inline asm implementation of HSD_IDInsertToTable once the C version matched.
Used inline helpers for hash and IDEntryAlloc, suggesting inlining was important to preserve the original instruction layout.
Used id % 0x65 for the hash calculation, corresponding to the assembly's modulo-by-101 sequence.
Matched linked-list traversal by loading the bucket head, looping while entry is non-NULL, breaking on id equality, and advancing through entry->next otherwise.
Matched insertion by setting the new entry's next pointer to the current bucket head and then assigning the bucket head to the new entry.
Removed peephole pragmas after the final matching rewrite, implying they were no longer necessary for this file.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching src/sysdolphin/baselib/id.c for any changes to IDEntryAlloc, hash, or HSD_IDInsertToTable.
Search for other baselib files where header macros were moved into local inline helpers to improve matching.
Search for PRs adding files to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED after removing NON_MATCHING asm blocks.
Compare object allocation helper patterns using HSD_ObjAlloc, __assert, and memset across sysdolphin/baselib.
